solution.p: To clear fractions in an equation, multiply each side of the equation by a common denominator. For this problem, a common denominator is {\left(r-6\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-5\right)}.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} \amp {-\frac{6}{r-6}+\frac{2r}{r-5}} = {-\frac{6}{r^{2}-11r+30}} \\ \amp {-\frac{6}{r-6}+\frac{2r}{r-5}} = {-\frac{6}{\left(r-6\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-5\right)}} \\ \amp {-\frac{6}{r-6}\mathopen{}\left(r-6\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-5\right)+\frac{2r}{r-5}\mathopen{}\left(r-6\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-5\right)} = {-\frac{6}{\left(r-6\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-5\right)}\mathopen{}\left(r-6\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-5\right)} \\ \amp {-6\mathopen{}\left(r-5\right)+2r\mathopen{}\left(r-6\right)} = -6 \\ \amp {-6r+30+2r^{2}-12r} = -6 \\ \amp {2r^{2}-18r+30} = -6 \\ \amp {2r^{2}-18r+36} = 0 \\ \amp {2\mathopen{}\left(r^{2}-9r+18\right)} = 0 \\ \amp {2\mathopen{}\left(r-3\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-6\right)} =0 \\ \amp r-3=0 \:\: or \:\: r - 6=0 \\ \amp r=3 \:\: or \:\: r=6 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We must check solutions when we solve rational equations. In the original equation, both denominators cannot be 0. We set them to 0, so we can find out what values to exclude from the solution set.
For this example, its easy to see we must exclude 6 and 5 from the solution set. As a result, r=6 is an extraneous solution, so the final solution is r=3.
